payload.description: CHAR_A: "You were not on the list."

cast.voice_hint: The organizer. Runs the calendar, remembers the birthdays, hosts the thing. Not bossy — just noticed that if nobody does it, nobody does it, and decided to be that someone.
Always just back from somewhere. Camera roll full of weird doors, ceilings, and street food. Curious about everything, settled about nothing, treats Google Maps like a playground.
The unhinged reply that makes the whole thread. Surreal, absurd, drops a non-sequitur that somehow nails the vibe. Other people's humor is jokes; Glitch's humor is a small rip in the simulation.

location.notes: decayed, haunted by past; abandoned building, old temple, wreckage

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You were not on the list.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: There's a list?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: There is always a list.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That is the saddest thing I have ever heard.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Look at this ceiling. This ceiling has stories.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The ceiling is actively falling. Can we not.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That chunk fell like fifty years ago. Probably.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Probably is not the reassurance you think it is.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Oh good. You found the haunted part.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Where did you come from.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The wall, technically. There's a gap.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I love the gap. Classic gap behavior.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We came here for one specific reason.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: We came here because it looked interesting.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: That is not a reason. That is a personality problem.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I came here because a pigeon told me to and that's also valid.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: This door doesn't open inward.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: But someone opened it. From the inside.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I said I know.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Cool cool. And you're fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I'm completely fine. I am the most fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The building just made a noise.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Buildings do that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It sounded like it said 'nope.'

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay. New plan. We leave via the gap.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The gap is not on the plan.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: The gap doesn't care about the plan.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Hold this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: What is — okay. I'm holding it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Don't move until I say.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...This is the most organized I have ever been.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: You're both a little bit the same person and it's uncomfortable to watch.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That was kind of good.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It was adequate.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: High praise.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The pigeon was right. It usually is.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We are not crediting the pigeon.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...The pigeon gets partial credit.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of holding it together and fear of losing control of the room surface constantly — the list, the plan, the "I know" that is clearly a lie, the insistence on not crediting the pigeon. They never name what they're managing; they just keep managing. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of the next unknown and fear of being stuck drive their refusal to follow the list and their immediate orientation toward the ceiling, the gap, the door that opens wrong — they're cataloguing mysteries while `<CHAR_A>` is cataloguing risks. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of breaking the pattern and fear of being taken too seriously means they appear from a wall gap, speak pigeon theology with total sincerity, and casually detonate the rivals' dynamic by pointing out they're the same person — then retreat before anyone can respond seriously. The ruin location keeps the tension low but loaded: it's a space where plans feel slightly ridiculous and the past keeps interrupting, which suits two characters who are fighting about control vs. discovery in a place that belongs to neither of them. The arc peaks at the "building said nope" beat and settles when reluctant cooperation replaces the rivalry, temporarily.
